data.title: Nvidia-Foxconn's Taiwan supercomputing center to be ready by H1 2026
data.description:
Foxconn Technology (OTCPK:FXCOF) said it plans to build Taiwan’s largest GPU cluster and its first supercomputing center deployment built on Nvidia's (NVDA) GB300 NVL72.

The Apple and Nvidia supplier — which is formally known as Hon Hai Precision (OTCPK:HNHAF) (OTCPK:HNHPF) — said the GB300 NVL72 systems are slated to come online in the first half of next year, marking an acceleration in Taiwan’s sovereign AI infrastructure development. 

Foxconn said the new AI factory infrastructure supports scalable enterprise-grade computing.

The announcement was made at the Hon Hai Tech Day (HHTD25). Hon Hai Technology Group has established Visionbay.ai, a dedicated business unit for AI supercomputing and cloud operations.

The 27-megawatt data center is worth about $1.4B, Reuters reported.

Foxconn and Nvidia did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha about the amount to be spent and the power capacity of the facility. 

Visionbay’s business model centers on an AI Factory ecosystem, offering GPU as a Service, or GPUaaS, compute leasing, NVIDIA-native software solutions, and a cloud-based AI App Store, according to the company.

By getting instant access to high-performance training, fine-tuning, development, and inference capabilities, enterprises can adopt AI with minimal barriers — powered by Nvidia's next-generation accelerated computing architecture, the company added.

Separately, Foxconn announced a partnership with OpenAI (OPENAI) to produce key components for its data centers within the U.S. The Taiwan-based contract manufacturer had teased the announcement related to OpenAI along with its third quarter results, where it provided a bright outlook for next year, pointing to AI-related demand as a big driver of growth in 2026.

data.title: Stock index futures rise day after Wall Street witnessed a volatile session
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday, a day after Nasdaq Composite saw a dramatic session due to a tech-led sell-off.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) +0.5%.

Wall Street ended lower on Thursday as a turbulent session sent the Nasdaq Composite on a dramatic round trip—from up more than 2% in the morning to down over 2% at the close.

"The S&P 500 initially climbed +1.93%, only to fade and close down -1.56% as doubts about AI valuations crept back in. That marked the biggest intra-day swing for the S&P since the six days of extreme market turmoil that followed the Liberation Day tariffs in early April. Adding to the negative backdrop for crypto were lingering questions over the crypto market structure bill that’s being worked on in Congress," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

To add to all of the drama on Thursday, investors also got the key September jobs data, which was initially delayed due to the U.S. government shutdown.

The data showed the U.S. labor market added 119K jobs in September, easily outstripping the consensus of 50K. The unemployment rate ticked up to 4.4% in the month, a tad higher than the expected 4.3%.

"The September U.S. employment report was finally released. This data’s quality has deteriorated dramatically—not because of the shutdown, but because survey response rates have been weak for some time, and the seasonal adjustment process in September has been a distortion in recent years. Payrolls rose, but so did the unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

To the day ahead now, there are a few Federal Reserve speakers lined up, including Fed Governor Michael Barr, Dallas Fed President Lorie Logan and Boston Fed President Susan Collins.

The PMI Composite Flash and the Consumer Sentiment will land during market hours. 

The manufacturing PMI is expected at 52.3 for the November flash versus 52.5 in the October final. The Consumer Sentiment index is expected to be at 50.5 in the November final, from 50.3 in the prior.

"While sentiment data can normally be ignored, the November Michigan consumer sentiment poll offers the entertainment of a political partisan breakdown. This helps identify how political bias may impact headlines—for instance, in the wake of recent elections and other political developments, Republican pessimism might arise from news flow rather than reality," Donovan added.

data.title: AnaptysBio sets $100M stock repurchase plan
data.description:
• AnaptysBio (ANAB) board has authorized an amended stock repurchase plan to buy back up to $100.0 million of its outstanding common stock.
  • This amendment is in addition to the $6.4 million that remained as of Nov. 20, 2025 under the current $75 million plan, of which Anaptys has repurchased 3.44M (11.2% shares outstanding before the start of this repurchase plan).
  • Excluding any additional potential purchases under this stock repurchase plan, Anaptys expects to end 2025 with ~$300 million in cash, cash equivalents and investments, including an anticipated accrual of a one-time $75 million commercial sales milestone in Q4 2025 due from GSK once Jemperli achieves $1 billion in worldwide net sales.
  • ANAB --2.25% premarket to $36.81
  • Source: Press Release

Germany’s manufacturing PMI falls to 48.4 points in November
data.description:
Manufacturing PMI in Germany decreased to 48.40 points in November from 49.60 points in October 2025.

Germany’s private sector saw a loss of momentum in November, with companies recording slower increases in both business activity and new orders, according to the latest ‘flash’ HCOB PMI® survey. After coming close to stabilizing the month before, employment meanwhile fell at a quicker rate amid a renewed decline in backlogs of work. Firms’ expectations for the year ahead were broadly unchanged, with stronger optimism among manufacturers being largely offset by weaker sentiment in the service sector.

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Trump drops 40% tariffs on Brazilian beef, coffee amid rising food prices
data.description:
President Donald Trump has signed an order to lift 40% tariffs on certain agricultural products from Brazil, including coffee, beef, fruits and cocoa, expanding efforts to bring down rising food prices in the U.S.

Trump said the order was due to progress in negotiations with Brazil and various officials recommending the move. 

The order applies to Brazilian imports to the U.S. on or after Nov. 13, and may require refunds of duties already collected.

The move follows a rollback of tariffs on several agricultural products to help ease high grocery prices for American consumers. 

Brazil has faced 50% tariffs for months. Trump raised them beyond his 10% across-the-board tariffs to penalize Brazil for prosecuting his ally Jair Bolsonaro, the country's former president.

To note, Brazil is the world's leading coffee exporter and a third of the coffee beans in the U.S. comes from the Latin American country. It is also the world's top beef exporter and a key beef supplier for burgers.
